# UC-22.41.1 · Encryption-at-rest coverage gap — unencrypted storage with regulated data

## Description

Produces a daily inventory of storage volumes that hold regulated data but are not encrypted at rest. Findings feed the remediation backlog and the evidence pack.

## Value

Unencrypted regulated data is the most common PCI 3.5 / HIPAA finding — preventing it from appearing on the audit spreadsheet is the goal.

## Implementation

(1) Ingest cloud-inventory telemetry; (2) tag volumes via data_classification.csv; (3) run daily; (4) on finding, auto-open a CISO review.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest cloud-inventory telemetry; (2) tag volumes via data_classification.csv; (3) run daily; (4) on finding, auto-open a CISO review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/cloud_storage/inventory splunk_server=* | where regulated_data="true"
| stats values(encryption_state) as state by volume_id region tenant
| where mvfind(state, "enabled")<0
| lookup data_classification.csv volume_id OUTPUT classification
| table tenant region volume_id classification state
```

Understanding this SPL

**Encryption-at-rest coverage gap — unencrypted storage with regulated data** — Unencrypted regulated data is the most common PCI 3.5 / HIPAA finding — preventing it from appearing on the audit spreadsheet is the goal.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• Filters the current rows with `where regulated_data="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **volume_id region tenant**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Filters the current rows with `where mvfind(state, "enabled")<0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).

Output fields: classification, region, state, tenant, volume_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar of encrypted vs unencrypted by tenant, table of findings, single-value '% of regulated volumes encrypted'.

## SPL

```spl
| rest /services/cloud_storage/inventory splunk_server=* | where regulated_data="true"
| stats values(encryption_state) as state by volume_id region tenant
| where mvfind(state, "enabled")<0
| lookup data_classification.csv volume_id OUTPUT classification
| table tenant region volume_id classification state
```

## Visualization

Stacked bar of encrypted vs unencrypted by tenant, table of findings, single-value '% of regulated volumes encrypted'.
